Zjd]d
dhdi
djdk
dkel
dldm
Implements a Jinja / Python combination lexer. The ``Lexer`` class
is used to do some preprocessing. It filters out invalid operators like
the bitshift operators we don't allow in templates. It separates
template code and python code in expressions.
literal_eval)
deque)
intern
pattern)
TemplateSyntaxError)
LRUCache)
Environment
Lexer
_lexer_cachez
\s+z
(\r\n|\r|\n)z7('([^'\\]*(?:\\.[^'\\]*)*)'|"([^"\\]*(?:\\.[^"\\]*)*)")z
    (
        0b(_?[0-1])+ # binary
    |
        0o(_?[0-7])+ # octal
    |
        0x(_?[\da-f])+ # hex
    |
        [1-9](_?\d)* # decimal
    |
        0(_?0)* # decimal zero
    )
    z
    (?<!\.)  # doesn't start with a .
    (\d+_)*\d+  # digits, possibly _ separated
    (
        (\.(\d+_)*\d+)?  # optional fractional part
        e[+\-]?(\d+_)*\d+  # exponent part
    |
        \.(\d+_)*\d+  # required fractional part
    )

Token.__str__ra
Test a token against a token expression.  This can either be a
        token type or ``'token_type:token_value'``.  This can only test
        against string values and types.
        Tr4
test

boolr
TokenStreamIteratorz`The iterator for tokenstreams.  Iterate over the stream
    until the eof token is reached.
    
TokenStreamN)
streamrJ
TokenStreamIterator.__init__r
__iter__/
TokenStreamIterator.__iter__c
currentr]
close
StopIteration
next
__next__2
TokenStreamIterator.__next__)
A token stream is an iterable that yields :class:`Token`\s.  The
    parser however does not iterate over it but calls :meth:`next` to go
    one token ahead.  The current active token is stored as :attr:`current`.
    )
	generatorr
iter
_iterr
_pushedr
closedrZ

z Push a token back to the stream.N)

zqPerform the token test and return the token if it matched.
        Otherwise the return value is `None`.
        N)

z8Like :meth:`next_if` but only returns `True` or `False`.N)

z|Go one token ahead and return the old one.
        Use the built-in :func:`next` instead of calling this directly.

OptionalLStripzWA special tuple for marking a point in the state that can have
    lstrip applied.

_Ruler
tokensZ
commandN)
Patternr
Union
Tuplerx
Class that implements a lexer for a given environment. Automatically
    created by the environment class, usually you don't have to do that.
    Note that the lexer is not automatically bound to an environment.
    Multiple environments can share the same lexer.
    r	
t t!f
d	|	
t,t-f

z:Calls tokeniter + tokenize and wraps it in a token stream.)
	tokeniterr
wrap)
tokenize\
Lexer.tokenize)
This is called with the stream as returned by `tokenize` and wraps
        every token in a :class:`Token` and converts the value.

msgr8
Lexer.wrapc
r*t d
t d |
This method tokenizes the text and returns the tokens in a
        generator. Use this method if you just want to tokenize a template.
        .. versionchanged:: 3.0
            Only ``\n``, ``\r\n`` and ``\r`` are treated as line
            breaks.
        N
